title: Quadlet support for -i and -t
body:
### Feature request description

Currently, Quadlets do not support the -i and -t flags, so any container started this way cannot be attached to in regard to stdin. This makes running certain applications (in my case, game servers) very inconvenient.

### Suggest potential solution

Add the options to the Container section

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
Maybe I'm missing something. But, Quadlet connects Podman to Systemd and as such, containers are executed in the background as services. As such, they are not expected to be connected to STDIN.
Can you please elaborate on your use case?

I did some further research, and I realized you can add the -i and -t flags to PodmanArgs.
Quadlets just make a systemd unit to run the podman commands/containers.

That's true. Any argument that does not have a corresponding key can be passed using the `PodmanArgs` key. But, still many (if not most) arguments do have corresponding keys to facilitate simpler usage.
So, while this request is not a must, the question of whether there should be a direct support for it is still valid. So, can you elaborate on your use case?

I use podman/quadlets for applications that use stdin for input. Primarily game servers (Minecraft, Terraria, Zomboid, etc), who are created in a way that you have to issue commands directly to the process' stdin. 
For just systemd you would need a socket of some sort, or some create tmux/screen sessions. Having direct stdin access is extremely difficult if you can't use -i and -t flags on podman.
